Figure 2: Comparison of predicted phase-space portraits for representative particles in the crystal lattice model across various dimensionality reduction methods.

Figure 3: Evolution of the global L2 error over time for the particles in the crystal lattice model . The plots illustrate (a) the reconstruction error and (b) the prediction error evaluated on the single target trajectory.
Table 2: Reconstruction and Prediction Errors for Tokamak System
Figure 4: Comparison of predicted 3D spatial trajectories for representative particles in the Tokamak system across various dimensionality reduction methods.

Figure 5: Evolution of the global L2 error over time for the charged particles in a Tokamak magnetic field system. The plots illustrate (a) the reconstruction error and (b) the prediction error evaluated on the single target trajectory.
Table 3: Reconstruction and Prediction Errors for Two-Stream System

Figure 6: Comparison of predicted phase-space snapshots capturing the formation and evolution of macroscopic vortices in the two-stream instability model across various dimensionality reduction methods.

Figure 7: Evolution of the global L2 error over time for the particles in the two-stream instability model. The plots illustrate (a) the reconstruction error and (b) the prediction error evaluated on the single target trajectory.
